clause_interactions.description: General liability cap may limit the effective value of indemnification obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify whether indemnification is carved out from general liability cap or subject to separate super-cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Indemnification becomes meaningless if capped below realistic claim values.

clause_interactions.description: Data breach indemnification may be rendered ineffective by a low general liability cap.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure data breach indemnification has a separate super-cap above the general liability cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Data breach costs (notification, remediation, regulatory fines) may far exceed general liability cap.

clause_interactions.description: IP infringement indemnification may be capped by general liability limitation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: IP indemnification should be carved out from general cap or subject to super-cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: IP litigation costs and injunction losses typically exceed standard liability caps.

clause_interactions.description: Consequential damages exclusion may prevent recovery of business losses beyond service credits.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that lost profits from SLA failures are not excluded as consequential damages.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Service credits provide only nominal remedy; real business losses are unrecoverable.

clause_interactions.description: Consequential damages exclusion may conflict with data breach indemnification if breach costs are classified as consequential.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Carve out data breach costs from consequential damages exclusion or define as direct damages.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Data breach notification, remediation, and regulatory fines treated as excluded consequential damages.

clause_interactions.description: Uptime commitments are only meaningful if backed by resolution time targets.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that resolution time targets are consistent with uptime percentage commitments.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: 99.9% uptime allows 8.7 hours annual downtime; resolution time must be shorter than this to be achievable.

clause_interactions.description: Uptime targets need corresponding response time commitments to detect and address issues promptly.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure P1 response time is measured in minutes, not hours, to support uptime targets.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Long response times mean outages are not addressed until uptime target is already breached.

clause_interactions.description: Service credits provide financial enforcement mechanism for uptime commitments.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify credit percentages are meaningful (10-30% of monthly fees) and tiered by severity.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Uptime targets without financial consequences are aspirational, not enforceable.

clause_interactions.description: SLA exclusions reduce the effective uptime commitment by excluding certain downtime events.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify exclusions are reasonable: planned maintenance windows, customer-caused issues. Not: all third-party failures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Broad exclusions reduce effective uptime from 99.9% to 95% or lower in practice.

clause_interactions.description: Termination for cause must not extinguish confidentiality obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify confidentiality survives termination for 3-5 years. Trade secrets indefinitely.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Termination releases party from confidentiality, allowing disclosure of sensitive information.

clause_interactions.description: Termination for convenience needs a structured wind-down period to avoid abrupt service cessation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify wind-down period (3-12 months) matches service complexity.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Convenience termination without wind-down creates cliff-edge service discontinuity.

clause_interactions.description: Even termination for cause needs orderly wind-down for service continuity.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Wind-down obligation should survive even for-cause termination, though period may be shorter.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: For-cause termination with no wind-down leaves customer without service or data.

clause_interactions.description: Insolvency termination may be triggered but wind-down obligations may be unenforceable against an insolvent entity.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Consider data escrow and alternative arrangements that do not depend on insolvent party cooperation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Wind-down provisions are meaningless if counter-party cannot perform due to insolvency.

clause_interactions.description: Termination fees effectively limit the practical utility of termination for convenience.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify fees are reasonable (declining scale) and do not negate the termination right.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Excessive termination fees make convenience termination financially impractical.

clause_interactions.description: Change of control termination right supplements assignment restrictions by covering indirect transfers.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both clauses needed: assignment covers direct transfer; change of control covers indirect via share acquisition.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Assignment restriction alone misses indirect transfers through share purchase or merger.

clause_interactions.description: Mutual confidentiality needs well-defined exceptions to be commercially workable.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify standard exceptions: public domain, prior knowledge, independent development, legally compelled disclosure.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Confidentiality without exceptions may prevent normal business operations and legal compliance.

clause_interactions.description: Residuals clause carves out information retained in unaided memory from confidentiality obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify residuals clause excludes trade secrets, personal data, and specific proprietary designs.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Broad residuals effectively nullify confidentiality for non-documented information.

clause_interactions.description: Audit rights provide verification mechanism for contractual security measures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify audit scope covers all security measures specified in the DPA.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Security measures are self-assessed without independent verification.

clause_interactions.description: Sub-processor clause must require flow-down of equivalent security measures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify sub-processor agreements require at least equivalent security measures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Data processed by sub-processors with lower security standards than primary processor.

clause_interactions.description: Deletion obligations must align with processing scope to ensure all data is addressed.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify deletion covers all categories of data in processing instructions, including backups.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Some data categories omitted from deletion, remaining with processor after contract end.

clause_interactions.description: Breach notification must be supported by security measures that enable detection.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify security measures include monitoring and detection capabilities to identify breaches.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Notification obligation meaningless if provider lacks capability to detect breaches.

clause_interactions.description: Cross-border transfers to non-adequate countries require Standard Contractual Clauses.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify appropriate SCC module executed with completed annexes and Transfer Impact Assessment.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: International transfer without legal mechanism violates GDPR Chapter V.

clause_interactions.description: SCCs require a Transfer Impact Assessment to determine if supplementary measures are needed.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify TIA conducted documenting destination country laws and identified supplementary measures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SCCs may be insufficient without supplementary measures identified by TIA.

clause_interactions.description: Where TIA identifies risks, supplementary measures must be implemented alongside SCCs.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify supplementary measures include: encryption, pseudonymisation, or access restrictions as needed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SCCs alone may not provide adequate protection against government access in destination country.

clause_interactions.description: Processor-to-processor SCCs also require TIA for compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Ensure TIA covers the specific P2P transfer scenario and destination country assessment.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Sub-processor transfers without TIA may be challenged by supervisory authority.

clause_interactions.description: SCCs require completed TOMs annex specifying technical and organisational measures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify TOMs annex is completed with specific, not generic, security measures.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Incomplete SCCs without TOMs annex are not legally valid.

clause_interactions.description: SCC sub-processor annex supplements DPA sub-processor provisions for international transfers.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify consistency between DPA sub-processor list and SCC sub-processor annex.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Inconsistent sub-processor lists between DPA and SCCs create compliance gaps.

clause_interactions.description: Data localisation requirements may conflict with cross-border transfer provisions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify that data localisation is not undermined by transfer provisions allowing processing elsewhere.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contract allows data to leave specified jurisdiction despite localisation requirement.

clause_interactions.description: Data subject rights assistance requires clear understanding of processing scope.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Processing instructions should define data categories to enable effective data subject rights response.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Processor cannot locate all relevant data for data subject request without clear processing scope.

clause_interactions.description: DPIA completion requires processor cooperation and information.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify processor has obligation to provide information needed for DPIA within reasonable timeframe.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Incomplete DPIA due to processor non-cooperation.

clause_interactions.description: Standard force majeure needs a termination right to prevent indefinite suspension.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify termination right available if force majeure continues beyond 60-90 days.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Parties trapped in non-performing contract during extended force majeure.

clause_interactions.description: Narrow force majeure provides tighter definition than standard, reducing abuse potential.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Choose narrow or standard based on risk appetite. Narrow better protects against FM abuse.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Standard FM definition may be too broad, allowing provider to avoid performance for predictable events.

clause_interactions.description: Pandemic-specific provision supplements standard FM with explicit pandemic coverage and mitigation.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Post-COVID, verify pandemic is either included with mitigation duties or explicitly addressed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Unclear pandemic coverage leads to disputes over FM applicability.

clause_interactions.description: Liability exclusion for force majeure events supplements the performance excuse.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify FM liability exclusion does not extend beyond the scope of the FM clause itself.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Liability exclusion broader than FM definition, creating unintended immunity.

clause_interactions.description: IP work product assignment requires clear background IP reservation to define boundary.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both clauses must exist with consistent definitions. Background IP schedule should be populated.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without background reservation, broad work product assignment may inadvertently capture pre-existing IP.

clause_interactions.description: Improvements clause supplements work product ownership by addressing enhancements to existing IP.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify improvements clause is consistent with work product ownership allocation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Improvements may fall into ownership gap between background and foreground IP.

clause_interactions.description: Licence-back enables provider to reuse general methodologies when work product is assigned to customer.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Licence-back should cover only general tools and methodologies, not customer-specific IP.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without licence-back, provider loses ability to reuse own methodologies. With broad licence-back, customer loses exclusivity.

clause_interactions.description: Non-assertion covenant supplements licence-back by preventing IP claims within the relationship.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify non-assertion covers both licensed and assigned IP within the agreement scope.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party may assert IP rights despite existing licence arrangement.

clause_interactions.description: Non-infringement warranty is only meaningful if backed by IP indemnification.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both warranty and indemnification should exist. Verify indemnification covers full defence costs.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Warranty breach claim exists but without indemnification, customer bears own defence costs.

clause_interactions.description: Fitness warranty needs SLA to define measurable standards for ongoing fitness.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Warranty covers initial delivery; SLA covers ongoing service quality. Both needed for complete coverage.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Gap between warranty period (typically 12 months) and ongoing SLA may leave unaddressed quality period.

clause_interactions.description: Services warranty supplements SLA by setting quality standard for manner of service delivery.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Warranty addresses how services are performed; SLA addresses measurable outcomes. Both needed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SLA measures outcomes but not quality of delivery process, or vice versa.

clause_interactions.description: Cyber insurance provides financial backing for data breach super-cap liability.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify insurance coverage equals or exceeds super-cap amount for data protection claims.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Super-cap liability exceeds insurance coverage, leaving gap in financial backing.

clause_interactions.description: Professional indemnity insurance provides financial backing for general liability cap.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify PI coverage at least matches the general liability cap amount.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Liability cap exceeds PI coverage, meaning provider cannot actually pay claims up to cap.

clause_interactions.description: General liability insurance provides financial backing for indemnification obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify insurance covers third-party claims that indemnification obligations address.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Indemnification obligations exist but provider lacks insurance to back them.

clause_interactions.description: Security audit rights provide verification mechanism for DPA security measures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify security audit scope covers all TOMs specified in the DPA.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Security measures claimed but never independently verified.

clause_interactions.description: Regulatory audit rights supplement DPA-specific audit rights for broader compliance verification.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both general regulatory and DPA-specific audit rights should be included.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: DPA audit rights may be narrower than regulatory requirements demand.

clause_interactions.description: Financial audit rights verify accuracy of usage-based billing.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: For variable pricing, financial audit must cover usage metrics and calculation methodology.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Usage-based billing is unverifiable without audit rights over usage data.

clause_interactions.description: Operational audit rights allow verification of SLA measurement and reporting accuracy.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify operational audit can access SLA measurement tools and underlying data.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SLA compliance self-reported by provider without independent verification.

clause_interactions.description: Governing law choice should be consistent with dispute resolution mechanism.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify arbitration seat is compatible with governing law for procedural efficiency.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Governing law and arbitration seat in different jurisdictions creates procedural complexity.

clause_interactions.description: Governing law should be paired with a venue clause for completeness.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify venue is consistent with governing law and practical for both parties.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Governing law in one jurisdiction, venue in another creates confusion and expense.

clause_interactions.description: Escalation procedure supplements mediation by providing structured pre-mediation resolution attempt.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify escalation tiers have time limits to prevent indefinite delay before mediation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without time limits, escalation becomes delay tactic before mediation can begin.

clause_interactions.description: Mediation provides cost-effective resolution attempt before arbitration.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify mediation has time limit (30-60 days) so it does not delay access to arbitration.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Mandatory mediation without time limit delays arbitration indefinitely.

clause_interactions.description: Litigation and arbitration clauses may conflict if both are specified without clear hierarchy.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Choose one forum. If both referenced, specify clear hierarchy and scope for each.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Parallel proceedings in different forums with potentially inconsistent outcomes.

clause_interactions.description: Affiliate assignment exception supplements general consent requirement.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify affiliate exception requires affiliate to meet same qualification standards.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Broad affiliate exception allows assignment to minimally qualified group company.

clause_interactions.description: Employee-specific non-solicitation provides more targeted protection than general mutual provision.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify scope covers directly involved personnel and period is reasonable (12 months).
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General mutual non-solicitation may be too broad or too narrow for practical protection.

clause_interactions.description: Authority to contract requires compliance with applicable laws to be meaningful.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both representations needed. Authority confirms capacity; compliance confirms legality.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Party has authority to contract but may be doing so in violation of applicable law.

clause_interactions.description: Accuracy of information representations are reinforced by corresponding warranty.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Warranty extends liability for inaccurate representations beyond contract execution.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Representation without warranty may limit remedies for inaccurate information.

clause_interactions.description: Anti-bribery compliance requires sanctions screening for effective compliance programme.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Both provisions needed. Anti-bribery addresses conduct; sanctions addresses counter-party status.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Anti-bribery compliance without sanctions screening misses entity-level risk.

clause_interactions.description: Export controls supplement sanctions by addressing technology and goods transfer restrictions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: For technology contracts, both export controls and sanctions compliance needed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Sanctions compliance without export controls may miss technology transfer restrictions.

clause_interactions.description: DORA service description must include data processing and storage locations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify service description specifies all data locations including sub-processor locations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Service description incomplete under DORA without data location transparency.

clause_interactions.description: DORA exit strategy must be supported by continuity arrangements during transition.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Exit strategy must include service continuity during transition period.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Exit strategy exists but service continuity during transition is not guaranteed.

clause_interactions.description: DORA audit rights supplement security testing by providing broader oversight capability.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Audit rights should enable verification of security testing results and methodology.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Security testing conducted but results not verifiable through audit.

clause_interactions.description: DORA subcontracting provisions require audit rights to extend to sub-contractors.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify audit rights explicitly cover sub-contractors providing critical or important functions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Subcontracting permitted but no ability to audit sub-contractor compliance.

clause_interactions.description: Incident reporting must be supported by continuity arrangements to maintain reporting during incidents.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify incident reporting capability is maintained during service disruption.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Major incident disrupts both service and incident reporting capability.

clause_interactions.description: NIS2 incident notification supplements GDPR breach notification for dual-regulated entities.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify notification process addresses both NIS2 (CSIRT) and GDPR (supervisory authority) timelines.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Meeting one notification requirement may not satisfy the other.

clause_interactions.description: NIS2 security requirements include vulnerability handling and patching as core obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Patching SLAs should be aligned with NIS2 security requirements (critical within 48 hours).
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General security requirements without specific patching obligations fail NIS2 compliance.

clause_interactions.description: Supply chain transparency requires access control to prevent unauthorised supply chain changes.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Access controls should prevent unauthorised changes to supply chain components.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Supply chain transparency undermined by inadequate access controls.

clause_interactions.description: Security certification provides third-party validation of NIS2 security requirements.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Certification should cover scope aligned with NIS2 requirements, not just generic security.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Certification scope does not cover all NIS2-required security measures.

clause_interactions.description: HIPAA safeguards must include breach detection capability to trigger notification obligations.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Safeguards should include monitoring to detect PHI breaches that trigger notification.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Breach notification obligation exists but no capability to detect qualifying breaches.

clause_interactions.description: HIPAA termination provisions address material breach of BAA including notification failures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify termination rights cover failure to comply with breach notification obligations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: No termination remedy for persistent HIPAA breach notification failures.

clause_interactions.description: HIPAA permitted use restrictions may be more restrictive than general DPA processing instructions.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: For dual GDPR-HIPAA scope, verify processing instructions satisfy both frameworks.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Processing permitted under GDPR DPA but prohibited under HIPAA permitted uses.

clause_interactions.description: Unlimited carve-outs remove specific risks from the general liability cap.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify carve-outs are appropriate: typically IP infringement, confidentiality breach, wilful misconduct, death/personal injury.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Too many carve-outs effectively negate the liability cap. Too few leave critical risks under-protected.

clause_interactions.description: Data protection super-cap provides elevated but still capped liability for data breaches.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Super-cap typically 2-5x base cap. Verify it covers regulatory fines, notification costs, and remediation.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Super-cap too close to base cap provides no meaningful additional protection for data claims.

clause_interactions.description: Liability floor prevents cap from becoming trivially small for low-value or variable-fee contracts.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Floor should be set at minimum 6-12 months of expected fees.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without floor, cap tied to variable fees may be negligibly small.

clause_interactions.description: Mutual vs asymmetric structure determines how the liability cap applies to each party.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify whether cap applies equally or asymmetrically and whether asymmetry is justified by risk profile.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Asymmetric cap without commercial justification indicates power imbalance.

clause_interactions.description: Aggregate vs per-incident structure determines how cap accumulates across multiple claims.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify both aggregate annual cap and per-incident cap are defined.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Per-incident only: cumulative claims exceed expectations. Aggregate only: single large claim consumes all capacity.

clause_interactions.description: Financial service credit mechanism provides detailed calculation methodology for SLA credits.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Credit mechanism should define: calculation formula, request process, application timing, and cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: SLA credits defined but calculation mechanism missing or unclear.

clause_interactions.description: Liquidated damages provide pre-agreed remedy for SLA failures beyond service credits.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify LDs are genuine pre-estimate of loss to avoid penalty clause risk.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: LDs set too high may be unenforceable as penalties; too low provides no incentive.

clause_interactions.description: Step-in rights provide alternative to termination for critical service failures.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Step-in should be available as remedy before termination, not only after.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Only option is termination, with no intermediate remedy for service failure.

clause_interactions.description: Escrow arrangement provides data and software protection against provider insolvency.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify escrow triggers include insolvency, cessation of support, and material uncured breach.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Insolvency termination right exists but data/software is inaccessible without escrow.

clause_interactions.description: Transition pricing defines costs during wind-down period.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Transition pricing should be at prevailing or slightly reduced rates, not punitive rates.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Wind-down obligation exists but at inflated pricing that penalises exit.

clause_interactions.description: Stranded costs provisions supplement termination fees for contract-specific investments.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify stranded costs are amortised and recoverable on early termination, not duplicative of termination fees.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Customer pays both termination fees and unamortised stranded costs, creating double penalty.

clause_interactions.description: Price lock period may conflict with CPI adjustment if both apply simultaneously.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify price lock takes precedence during lock period, with CPI adjustment only after lock expires.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Ambiguity about whether CPI adjustment applies during price lock period.

clause_interactions.description: Most-favoured customer clause supplements benchmarking by providing automatic price protection.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: MFC provides real-time protection; benchmarking provides periodic market comparison. Both are valuable.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Benchmarking without MFC means customer only discovers overpricing periodically.

clause_interactions.description: Late payment interest provides incentive for timely payment within net-30 terms.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Interest rate should be commercially reasonable (base rate + 2-4%).
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Net-30 terms without late interest incentive leads to systematic late payment.

clause_interactions.description: Open book accounting provides ongoing transparency supplementing periodic cost verification.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Open book should cover all cost categories used in pricing calculations.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Cost verification right without access to underlying data is difficult to exercise.

clause_interactions.description: Parent guarantee provides broader financial backing complementing specific performance bond.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: One or both may be appropriate depending on counter-party financial strength.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Neither guarantee nor bond means contractual obligations have no financial backing.

clause_interactions.description: Gain-sharing incentivises continuous improvement complementing benchmarking's market alignment.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Gain-sharing motivates efficiency; benchmarking ensures market alignment. Both drive value.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Benchmarking without gain-sharing means provider has no incentive to drive efficiencies.

clause_interactions.description: Net-30 payment terms need late interest to incentivise compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify interest clause specifies: rate, start date, compounding, and application process.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Payment terms without interest incentive are routinely exceeded.

clause_interactions.description: Price adjustment mechanism supplemented by MFC to prevent adjustments above market.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: MFC should cap adjustments at rates offered to comparable customers.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Price adjustments may exceed market rates without MFC protection.

clause_interactions.description: Customer and provider indemnification may conflict when both parties contributed to a loss.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Include contributory fault mechanism to apportion liability for joint-cause incidents.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Mutual claims with no apportionment mechanism leads to litigation.

clause_interactions.description: DPA personnel confidentiality supplements general confidentiality for data protection compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify DPA confidentiality covers all personnel processing personal data, including temporary staff.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: General confidentiality may not meet GDPR Article 28(3)(b) specific personnel requirement.

clause_interactions.description: Records of processing provide evidence trail for processing instruction compliance.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: ROPA should be consistent with and traceable to documented processing instructions.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Processing records do not match instructions, indicating unauthorised processing.

clause_interactions.description: Title warranty provides assurance that IP assignment is valid and unencumbered.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Title warranty should confirm: clear ownership, no encumbrances, no prior assignments or licences.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: IP assigned without title warranty may be subject to prior claims.

clause_interactions.description: Warranty of compliance extends representation into ongoing obligation throughout contract term.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Representation covers execution date; warranty covers ongoing performance period. Both needed.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Representation only covers execution date, leaving ongoing compliance unaddressed.

clause_interactions.description: Milestone payments need acceptance criteria to determine when payment is earned.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Each milestone should have defined acceptance criteria and testing period before payment is triggered.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Payment triggered by milestone completion without quality verification.

clause_interactions.description: Additional insured status provides customer direct access to provider's insurance coverage.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify additional insured endorsement on policy, not just contractual commitment.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Contractual commitment to add as additional insured but no actual policy endorsement.

clause_interactions.description: Penalty cap limits maximum accumulation of liquidated damages.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Verify penalty cap is reasonable (10-20% of affected deliverable value) and includes termination right at cap.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: No cap means liquidated damages can accumulate beyond contract value.

clause_interactions.description: Earn-back mechanism supplements service credits by incentivising provider improvement.
clause_interactions.review_guidance: Earn-back should require sustained above-target performance (3+ months) before credit recovery.
clause_interactions.risk_if_misaligned: Without earn-back, provider may prefer termination over continuous credit outflow.
